PDO: To strengthen the Government's program for reducing risks resulting from adverse natural events.
Description: Disaster Risk Management DPL w/Catastrophe Deferred Draw Down Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of national emergency exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by the Borrower's presidential decree in accordance with the Borrower's legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn down upon occurrence of a natural disaster resulting in a declaration by decree of a state of national disaster by the president of Colombia, in accordance with Law Decree (Decreto Ley) No. 919 of 1989, or as may be amended by the Borrower at the time of disbursement.8 Since the law was passed in 1989 there have been nine declarations of emergency: five for municipal emergencies, three for departmental emergencies, and one for a national emergency. The disbursement for this operation would be triggered only by a declaration of a national disaster.

PDO: To enhance the government’s capacity to implement its disaster risk management program for natural disasters.
Description: Costa Rica Catastrophe Deferred Draw Down Option (CAT DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency exists in the Borrower’s territory as a result of a Natural Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown Triggers. Funds may be drawn down upon occurrence of a natural disaster resulting in a declaration of a state of emergency by the President o f Costa Rica through an Executive Decree, in accordance with Law No. 8488 of January 1 1,2006.

PDO: To enhance the Government's capacity to implement its disaster risk management program for natural disasters.
Description: Catastrophe Development Policy Loan Deferred Draw Down Option (DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a national and/or Regional state of emergency (estado de calamidad publica) exists in the Borrower’s territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by a Borrower’s presidential decree and ratified by the Borrower’s Congress in accordance with the Borrower’s legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown Triggers. Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a national and/ or regional state of emergency’ (Estudo de Culumidud Pziblicu), by Presidential Decree, under the Public Order Law 7, Article 14. The National Congress must ratify the declaration of the state of emergency.

PDO: The development objective of the proposed operation is to strengthen the Government’s capacity to mobilize resources in the case of disaster and to promote risk reduction.
Description: Catastrophe Development Policy Loan DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a national State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) exists in the Borrower’s territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by the Borrower, through a Decreto Supremo (Presidential Decree), all in accordance with the Borrower’s legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown Triggers. Funds may be withdrawn upon the declaration of a State of Emergency10 (Estado de Emergencia) by a Presidential Decree (see Annex 3 for more details). Taking into account that the law (PD No. 069-2005-PCM) enables the use of this declaration in a catastrophic disaster, it has been agreed between the Government and the Bank that the declaration is a valid drawdown trigger for the DPL with a CATDDO. A disaster is defined as one that is generated by phenomena that are either natural/geological (e.g., earthquakes, tsunamis, volcanoes, landslides, etc.) and/or hydro-meteorological (e.g., floods, hurricanes, El Niño/La Niña, storms, surges, etc.).

PDO: To enhance the Government of El Salvador's capacity to implement its Disaster Risk Management Program for natural disasters.
Description: Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency (estado de emergencia) exists in the Borrower’s territory, or in a portion thereof, as a result of a natural disaster, which has been declared by the Borrower’s National Assembly, upon request by the Borrower’s President, or directly by the Borrower’s President, through a Executive Decree, if the National Assembly is not in session, in accordance with the Borrower’s legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) by Legislative or Presidential Decree (the latter is prescribed by law in case the Legislative Assembly is not in session). As indicated before, the National Commission is responsible, among other duties, for proposing to the President of El Salvador the declaration of a State of Emergency to protect the life and assets of the affected population, ensuring at the same time public safety and immediate response with the support of civil and military authorities, firefighters and humanitarian organizations. Such a declaration is a valid drawdown trigger for the DPL with a CAT DDO as long as it is the result of a natural event. According to the Salvadorian law, this is the result of an eminent disaster risk or a dangerous situation generated by a disaster that may affect people, their assets, public services, or ecosystems.
Disasters that have triggered the Cat DD: precipitation levels from a tropical depression

PDO: To enhance the GoP’s capacity to implement its Disaster Risk Management Program for natural disasters.
Description: Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) exists in the Borrower’s territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by the Borrower, through an Executive Decree (Decreto Ejecutivo), all in accordance with the Borrower’s legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a state of emergency (estado de emergencia) in the Borrower’s territory, as a result of a natural disaster, under terms and conditions specified in the Loan Agreement. The Government of Panama has requested not to include Pandemics in the definition of the State of Emergency. Change during the restructuring: Under the restructuring, the loan agreement would be amended to deem public health-related events such as pandemics of this nature emergencies eligible to trigger disbursement.

PDO: To enhance the capacity of the Government of the Philippines to manage the impacts of natural disasters.
Description: Disaster Risk Management Development Loan with a CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless a State of Calamity exists in the Borrower’s territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by the Borrower’s President through a Proclamation of Calamity duly published in the Official Gazette in accordance with the Borrower’s legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown Triggers. Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a State of Calamity by the President. The Proclamation of Calamity should be in accordance with the DRRM Act, and should also be duly published in the Official Gazette. The guidelines prescribe that the declaration remains effective for a period of one year; this timeframe may be extended or shortened by the President, as necessary.

PDO: To strengthen the Government's program for reducing risks resulting from adverse natural events.
Description: Second Disaster Risk Management DPL with Catastrophe Deferred Draw Down Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of National Disaster exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown Trigger. Funds may be drawn down by the GoC upon occurrence of a disaster triggered by a natural event, but specifically excluding disasters triggered by anthropogenic causes, resulting in a declaration by decree of a national situation of disaster by the President of Colombia, in accordance with Article 56 of Law 1523 of 2012, or as may be amended by the Borrower.17 Since 1989, there have been eleven declarations of disaster (under the Law 919 of 198918): five at the municipal level, four at the departmental level, but only two for a national disaster, as illustrated in Table 6. The disbursement for this operation would be triggered only by a declaration of a national situation of disaster19. Note that these are the same criteria as for the first DPL with a CAT DDO, which was approved by the Board on December 18, 2008. Article 55 establishes that for the effect of the present law, a disaster is the result that triggered the manifestation of one or more natural or unintentional anthropogenic events, that in finding conditions of vulnerability in persons, property, infrastructure, livelihoods, service provision, or environmental resources, cause damages or losses of life, materials, economic or environmental assets, generating an intense, severe and extended disturbance, in the normal functioning of society, which requires the intervention of the State and the National System to implement actions of response, rehabilitation and reconstruction.

PDO: To enhance the capacity of the Government of Sri Lanka (GoSL) to be more resilient to the impacts of natural disasters.
Description: Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (DPL with DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster, and which has been declared by the Borrower through a Proclamation of the President, in accordance with the provisions of the Disaster Management Act.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon declaration of a state of emergency in the Borrower’s territory, as a result of a natural disaster, under terms and conditions specified in the Financing Agreement. Under this DPL, to draw down funds the President must declare a State of Disaster, in accordance with provisions of the DM Act. After the President makes a declaration of emergency, the MDM and NCDM will coordinate with the Ministry of Finance to make a decision on triggering the drawdown.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of Seychelles Disaster Risk Management policy and reform agenda and enhance its capacity to efficiently respond to disasters.
Description: Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a state of emergency as established by the Constitution of 1993. According to Seychelles legislation, a state of emergency can be invoked in the event of an eminent natural or man-made disaster. The drawdown trigger for the DPL with Cat DDO is limited to declaration of a state of emergency resulting from a natural disaster.

PDO: To strengthen the legal and institutional framework to contribute toward the reduction of Peru's fiscal and physical vulnerability to disasters.
Description: Peru Second DPL with a Cat DDO
Note: In Peru originally commitment was of 400M, but only 70M were requested and disbursed.
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a national State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by the Borrower, through a Decreto Supremo (Supreme Decree), all in accordance with the Borrower's legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be withdrawn upon the declaration of a State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) through a Supreme Decree. The regulation that implements the SINAGERD DRM law includes Title VI: Declaration of State of Emergency and three articles: Article 67–Declaring a State of Emergency due to a Disaster, Article 68–Procedures to declare a State of Emergency, and Article 69- Extending a State of Emergency.

PDO: To enhance the technical and financial capacity of the Government of the Philippines to reduce disaster risk and manage the socio-economic and fiscal impacts of natural disasters.
Description: Second Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a CAT-DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, that a State of Calamity exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster which has been declared by the Borrower's President through a Proclamation of Calamity duly published in the Official Gazette in accordance with the Borrower's legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The trigger for drawdown will be that a State of Calamity exists in the Borrower’s territory as a result of a natural disaster, which has been declared by the President through a Proclamation of Calamity duly published in the Official Gazette, in accordance with the DRRM Act. A “state of calamity” is defined in the DRRM Act as a condition involving mass casualty and/or major damages to property, disruption of means of livelihoods, roads and normal way of life of people in the affected areas as a result of the occurrence of natural or human-induced hazard.

PDO: To strengthen Serbia's institutional and legal framework to effectively manage the physical and fiscal impact of natural hazards.
Description: Disaster Risk Management DPL-CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that an emergency situation exists in the Borrower's territory as a result of a natural disaster that has been declared by the Borrower, through its central government, in accordance with the Borrower's relevant legislation, including specifically the Law on Reconstruction Following Natural and Other Hazards.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon declaration of a state of emergency in the borrower’s territory, as a result of a natural disaster, under terms and conditions specified in the Financing Agreement. Under this DPL, to draw down funds, the Government must declare a state of emergency, in accordance with provisions of the Law on Emergency Situations and Civil Protection (2009). Only a state of emergency declared by the National Cabinet will be eligible. After the Government makes a declaration of emergency, it will coordinate with the MoF to make a decision on triggering the drawdown.

PDO: To support the Government of the Dominican Republic in (a) strengthening institutions for improved climate and disaster resilience in priority sectors and (b) establishing mandatory regulations for climate and disaster risk reduction in public investment and construction works.
Description: Dominican Republic DRM Development Policy Loan with a Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) has been declared to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown triggers. Loan Proceeds may be withdrawn upon a declaration of a State of Emergency by the President that has been declared to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe, which may include public health-related events, through an Executive Decree in accordance with Article 128(1)(g), 262, 265, and 266 of the DR’s Constitution of 2015, under terms and conditions specified in the Loan Agreement.

PDO: To strengthen Romania’s institutional and legal framework to effectively manage the physical, social and fiscal impacts of natural disasters and climate change
Description: Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied that in response to a Catastrophe Event that NCSES has issued a decision in accordance with Emergency Ordinance No.21/2004, as subsequently amended, mobilizing financial and/or other resources to respond to such an event.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The key legislation regulating the management of the emergency situations is the Government Emergency Ordinance No. 21/2004 on the National Emergency Situations Management System (Sistemul Național de Management al Situațiilor de Urgență), as subsequently amended , the Government Decision No. 557/2016 regarding the management of all types of risk and its secondary legislation the GD No. 94/2014 on certain measures for emergency situation management. Catastrophe Event means, for the purpose of this Program, an imminent or occurring emergency situation that requires the Borrower to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources, pursuant to the Borrower’s Emergency Ordinance No.21/2004, as subsequently amended. For the purposes of the loan, a Catastrophe Event, in addition to the natural hazard risks, may include, the public-health related emergencies, such as the threat or outbreak of fatal or highly infectious diseases including endemics, epidemics, or pandemics or the spread of biological or chemical agents. But a Catastrophe Event does not include any emergency that is predominantly political, technological, security, military, or fiscal in nature.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of Kenya’s institutional, technical, and financial capacities to manage the impact of climate and disaster risks.
Description: Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Credit with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (Cat
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied that, in response to a Disaster, a competent government agency or official has issued: (i) an emergency order or declaration under applicable law; or (ii) decrees or orders mobilizing financial or other resources to respond to such an event.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn in response to a declaration of disaster, by the GoK in accordance with national law. In addition to natural hazard risks, a disaster may include, public health related emergencies, such as the threat or outbreak of fatal or highly infectious diseases including endemics, epidemics, or pandemics. For the purposes of the DPF, a disaster does not include any emergency that is predominantly political, technological, security, military, or fiscal in nature.

PDO: To help strengthen Guatemala’s legal, institutional and financial framework to manage the impact of adverse natural events and climate risk
Description: Guatemala DRM Development Policy Loan with CAT DDO II
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency (estado de calamidad p4blica) exists in the Borrower's territory related to a natural catastrophe, including public-health related events caused by epidemics, pandemics, and emerging diseases, which has been declared by the Borrower in accordance with its legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The trigger for drawdown is the declaration of a national or regional state of emergency (Estado de Calamidad Pública) by Government Decree ratified by Congress. In the event of a major disaster, the National Emergency Coordination Center of Operations, a branch of CONRED, proposes the declaration of a state of emergency at the national and/or regional level to the executive branch. In accordance with Public Order Law, Article 14 (Law No. 7, 1975), the President, in a meeting with the Council of Ministers, reviews the proposal and can declare a state of national or regional emergency as appropriate. This declaration has to be ratified by Congress. Subsequently, the declaration is published in the official gazette, allowing the GoG to trigger the CAT DDO (Annex 5 includes an overview of past national declarations of emergency and the impacts of the respective disasters and in Guatemala). While no declaration of a state of emergency has been declared in Guatemala to date for exclusively health-related events, such a declaration is also an eligible trigger for drawdown and would have to follow the same procedures described above.

PDO: To strengthen the institutional and financial capacity of the Government of Malawi with respect to its multisectoral approach on disaster and climate risk management.
Description: Malawi Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Financing with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that (a) the President of the Recipient has declared a "State of Disaster" in accordance with Article 32 of the Malawi Disaster Preparedness and Relief Act of December 17, 1991, or such other law applicable to disaster risk management as may be in force in Malawi al the time, and (b) there is an imminent or occurring (i) natural disaster as a result of natural geological phenomena, including but not limited to earthquakes, volcanoes and landslides, hydro-meteorological phenomena such as floods, droughts, tropical cyclones, and El Nino /La Nina events, or (ii) health disaster as a result of a plague or epidemic of disease. that threatens the life or well­being of a community.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that (a) the President of the Recipient has declared a “State of Disaster” in accordance with Article 32 of Malawi’s Disaster Preparedness and Relief Act (DPRA) of December 17, 1991, or such other law applicable to DRM as may be in force in Malawi at the time; and (b) there is an imminent or occurring (i) natural disaster as a result of natural geological phenomena, including but not limited to earthquakes, volcanoes and landslides, hydrometeorological phenomena such as floods, droughts, tropical cyclones, and El Niño /La Niña events, or (ii) health disaster as a result of a plague or epidemic of disease that threatens the life or well-being of a community.

PDO: (a) Strengthening the institutional and legal framework to ensure risk-informed sectoral and territorial planning; and (b) Increasing the financial capacity to manage impacts associated with disaster and climate-related shocks.
Description: Cabo Verde Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Financing with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Calamity has been declared to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The Cat DDO has a “soft” trigger which means that Loan and Credit Proceeds may be withdrawn upon a declaration of a “State of Calamity” that has been issued through a Resolution of the Council of Ministers to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe, under article 20 of Law no. 12/VIII/2012 of March 7th, and under no. 2 of article 265 of Cabo Verde’s Constitution.

PDO: To strengthen Grenada’s (a) disaster and climate resilience policy framework, and (b) fiscal risk management associated with natural hazards.
Description: Grenada Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Credit with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Government Declaration has been issued to respond to a National Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The Cat DDO funds may be drawn upon the occurrence of a national disaster resulting in an official Government Declaration. “Government Declaration” means a declaration made by the Prime Minister and published in the Recipient’s Government Gazette (or by notices affixed to public buildings or distributed amongst the public, or by announcement on the radio or otherwise, if publication in the Gazette is impossible or impractical) that a National Disaster has occurred in the territory of the Recipient or any part thereof, all in accordance with the provisions of the Recipient’s National Disaster (Emergency Powers) Act of 1984. “Disaster” means an imminent or occurring situation due to a natural or health events in accordance with the Recipient’s National Disaster (Emergency Powers) Act of 1984, including but not limited to earthquakes, hurricanes, floods, fires, outbreak of pestilence or of infectious disease, or other calamity, as a result of which supplies and services essential to the life of the community are likely to be endangered. For clarity, any emergency that is predominantly political, technological, security, military, or fiscal in nature, is excluded.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of Madagascar’s institutional, technical and financial capacity to manage disaster and climate-related risks.
Description: Madagascar Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdow
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that the Recipient's Declaration of a National Disaster has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Under this DPF, funds may be drawn down upon a “Declaration of a National Disaster” (Déclaration de Sinistre National), a declaration by the Recipient of a national disaster due exclusively to a catastrophic disaster generated by an eligible natural catastrophe, including inter alia: earthquake, tsunamis, volcanoes, landslides, drought, tropical cyclones, floods, and storms surge or public health emergency that has caused, or is likely to cause imminently, a major adverse economic and/or social impact on the Recipient in accordance with the Recipient’s Law No. 215-031 “Politique Nationale de Gestion des Risques et des Catastrophes” (National Disaster Risk Management Policy).

PDO: To support the Royal Government of Bhutan in enhancing its technical and institutional capacity to manage the risk of climate change and natural disasters including disease outbreaks.
Description: Bhutan Development Policy Financing with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied based on evidence satisfactory to it, that in response to a Natural Disaster: (a) (i) a declaration of disaster has been made pursuant to the provisions of the Disaster Management Act; or (ii) the Prime Minister, in his capacity as the ex-officio Chairperson of the NOMA has declared a disaster; or (b) the MoF has, on the recommendation of the NDMA acting under the relevant provisions of the Disaster Management Act, issued in accordance witl1 Section 66 of the Public Finance Act, 2007, an order to authorize use of public funds to be drawn down for the purpose of mobilizing financial resources to respond to such a Natural Disaster; each in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown triggers. No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied based on evidence satisfactory to it, that in response to a Natural Disaster21: (a) (i) a declaration of disaster has been made pursuant to the provisions of the Disaster Management Act; or (ii) the Prime Minister, in his capacity as the ex-officio Chairperson of the NDMA has declared a disaster; or (b) the MoF has, on the recommendation of the NDMA acting under the relevant provisions of the Disaster Management Act, issued in accordance with Section 66 of the Public Finance Act, 2007, an order to authorize use of public funds to be drawn down for the purpose of mobilizing financial resources to respond to such a Natural Disaster; each in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate. “Natural Disaster” means, for the purposes of this Cat DDO, an imminent or occurring crisis or emergency situation arising from a cyclone, flood, storm, tsunami, drought, earthquake, volcanic eruption, plague or epidemic, or any other natural hazard, including a public-health related event, that requires the Recipient to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources under its applicable legal or administrative frameworks.

PDO: To support the Government of Morocco in (a) strengthening the country’s institutional capacity to address the adverse financial impact of disasters and climate-related shocks, and (b) strengthening Morocco's institutional framework for disaster and climate-related risk management.
Description: Morocco Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Borrower's Declaration of Catastrophic Event has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The Morocco Cat DDO was restructured on March 24, 2020, to expand the definition of the disbursement trigger to add public health-related emergencies, in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and following the request of the Government.

PDO: Strengthen Honduras's institutional and financial framework to manage the risk of adverse natural events including disease outbreaks
Description: Honduras DRM Development Policy Credit with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (Cat DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that an Emergency has been declared by the Recipient in accordance with the Recipient's DRM Legislation, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe including a natural disaster, a disease outbreak and/or an epidemiological emergency in human population (i.e. epidemics and/or pandemics).
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be withdrawn when an emergency is imminent or has already occurred in the Recipient’s territory related to the adverse impact of a natural catastrophe, including disease outbreaks and epidemics in the human population, which has been declared by the Recipient through a Presidency of the Council of Ministers PCM (Presidencia del Consejo de Ministros) Executive Decree and published in the Recipient’s Official Gazette, all in accordance with the provisions of the Recipient’s legislation: Articles 42 and 45 of the “Disaster Risk Management National System Act” (SINAGER Law) approved by Legislative Decree 151‐2009, and its Regulation approved by Executive Decree 032‐2010 (see Annex 7).

PDO: To support the government in enhancing its technical and institutional capacity to manage the risk of climate change and natural disasters including disease outbreaks
Description: Nepal DRM Development Policy Credit with CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Disaster has been declared by the Recipient either: (a) by the Ministry of Home Affairs, as specified in the Government of Nepal (Allocation of Business) Rules of 23rd of February of 2018, Schedule 2, Section 8, points 18 and 19, in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe, or (b) by the Ministry of Health and Population, as per the provisions of the Public Health Service Act of 2018, Chapter 6, Section 48, in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate, to respond to an imminent or occurring health-related shock.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn down by the Recipient either (i) upon imminent or occurring any adverse natural event in the country followed by a declaration of disaster by the Ministry of Home Affairs, the leading disaster management entity as specified in the Government of Nepal (Allocation of Business) Rules of 23rd of February of 2018, Schedule 2, Section 8, page 15, points 18 and 19, or (ii) upon imminent or occurring health-related shock in the country followed by a declaration of state of public health emergency by the Ministry of Health and Population as per the provisions of the Public Health Service Act of 2018, Chapter 6, section 48, each in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate.

PDO: To enhance the Recipient's regulatory framework and institutional capacity to: (i) manage and reduce the risks from natural disasters and climate change; and (ii) manage public debt.
Description: Vanuatu Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Grant with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Optio
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that, in response to a Natural Catastrophe: (a) the Recipient's President has issued, in accordance with the Recipient's applicable law, an order to declare that a state of emergency exists in the Recipient's territory in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or (b) the Recipient's minister responsible for finance has issued, in accordance with the provisions of the Public Finance and Economic Management Act, an order to authorize amounts to be drawn down from the public fund for the purposes of alleviating a financial emergency in relation to such Natural Catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Originally not included but the financing agreement was amended: "Natural Catastrophe" means, for the purposes of this Agreement, an imminent or ' occurring emergency situation caused by an earthquake, tsunami, cyclone, storm, flood, volcanic eruption, drought, bush fire or any other natural hazard, or any public health-related event, that requires the Recipient to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources." (https://wbdocs.worldbank.org/wbdocs/component/drl?objectId=090224b08780c1ea&standalone=true&Reload=1619530515318&__dmfClientId=1619530515318&respositoryId=WBDocs&__dmfTzoff=240).

PDO: To enhance the Maldives' financial capacity to effectively manage the human, physical and fiscal impact of climate change, natural disasters including disease outbreaks.
Description: Maldives Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option and Pandemic Emerg
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Disaster has been declared by the Recipient either (a) by the Chairperson of the NDM Council on the recommendation of the National Disaster Management Authority as per the provisions of the Disaster Management Act read with section 3, Volume 1 of the National Emergency Operations Plan; or (b) by the Recipient's Minister of Health as per the provisions of the Public Health Protection Act, read with Section 1 of the HEOP; or (c) by the President under Article 253 of the Constitution, each through the form of a public notice/announcement in such manner as the Recipient deems fit, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe (including any health-related shock).
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn down based on evidence satisfactory to the Bank, that a State of Disaster has been declared by the Recipient either (a) by the Chairperson of the NDM Council on the recommendation of the National Disaster Management Authority as per the provisions of the Disaster Management Act read with section 3, Volume 1 of the National Emergency Operations Plan; or (b) by the Recipient’s Minister of Health as per the provisions of the Public Health Protection Act, read with Section 1 of the HEOP; or (c) by the President under Article 253 of the Constitution, each through the form of a public notice/announcement in such manner as the Recipient deems fit, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe (including any health-related shock).

PDO: To enhance the Recipient's regulatory framework and institutional capacity to: (i) manage and reduce the risks from natural disasters and climate change; and (ii) to better manage public debt and enhance debt transparency.
Description: Vanuatu Second Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Grant with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that, in response to a Natural Catastrophe: (a) the Recipient's President has issued, in accordance with the Recipient's Constitution, an order to declare that a state of emergency exists in the Recipient's territory in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or (b) the Recipient's President has issued, in accordance with the Recipient's Disaster Risk Management Act, an order to declare that a state of emergency exists in the Recipient's territory in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or (c) the Recipient's Minister of Finance has issued, in accordance with the provisions of the Public Finance and Economic Management Act, an order to authorize amounts to be drawn down from the public fund for the purposes of alleviating a financial emergency in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or (d) The Recipient's Minister of Health has issued, in accordance with the provisions of the Public Health Act, an order that a state of public health emergency exists in the Recipient's territory.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: a) the Recipient’s President has issued, in accordance with the Recipient’s Constitution, an order to declare that a state of emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or b) the Recipient’s President has issued, in accordance with the Recipient’s Disaster Risk Management Act, an order to declare that a state of emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or c) the Recipient’s Minister of Finance has issued, in accordance with the provisions of the Public Finance and Economic Management Act, an order to authorize amounts to be drawn down from the public fund for the purposes of alleviating a financial emergency in relation to such Natural Catastrophe; or d) The Recipient’s Minister of Health has issued, in accordance with the provisions of the Public Health Act, an order that a state of public health emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of Colombia’s program to manage risk resulting from adverse natural events, including disease outbreaks
Description: Third Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Presidential Declaration has been issued in response to a Disaster in all or part of the Borrower's territory.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Upon approval of the operation, effectiveness of the Financing Agreement, and the submission of a signed withdrawal application, the loan proceeds may be drawn down at any time, provided, however, that a pre-specified trigger—linked to a declaration of a state of emergency resulting from a natural catastrophe or health emergency—has been activated.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of the Philippines' institutional and financial capacity to manage risks from climate change, natural disasters, and disease outbreaks.
Description: Fourth Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that (i) a proclamation declaring the State of Calamity has been issued by the Borrower's President due to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe and/or a public health emergency; and/or (ii) a proclamation declaring State of Public Health Emergency has been issued by the Borrower's President due to a public health emergency.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be draw down upon the Borrower issuing a Presidential proclamation for an imminent or occurring (a) State of Calamity (defined as "a situation resulting from a disaster caused by one or more hazards as specified in the Borrower's Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act OF 2010" but excluding events addressed in the proclamation dated October 12, 2021) or (b) public health emergency.

PDO: To consolidate and expand Honduras' institutional, regulatory, policy and financial framework to manage disaster and climate-related risks, including disease outbreaks.
Description: Honduras Second DRM Development Policy Credit with Deferred Drawdown Option (Cat DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that an Emergency has been declared by the Recipient in accordance with the Recipient's DRM Legislation, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe including a natural disaster, a disease outbreak and/or an epidemiological emergency in human population (i.e. epidemics and/or pandemics).
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Cat DDO drawdown trigger. Funds may be withdrawn when an emergency is imminent or has already occurred in the Recipient’s territory related to the adverse impact of a natural catastrophe, including disease outbreaks and epidemics in the human population, which has been declared by the Recipient through a Presidency of the Council of Ministers (Presidencia del Consejo de Ministros, PCM).
Disasters that have triggered the Cat DD: Tropical storm causing flooding and landslides

PDO: To enhance the Government of Panama's technical and institutional capacity to manage the disaster risk resulting from the occurrence of natural and health-related hazards, including the adverse effects of climate change and disease outbreaks.
Description: Second Panama Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) exists in the Borrower's territory to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe which has been declared by the Borrower, through a Cabinet Resolution (Resolucion de Gabinete), all in accordance with the Borrower's legislation.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a State of Emergency in the Panama’s territory, as a result of an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe generated by natural phenomena or health related events (such as hurricanes, earthquakes, pandemics, etc.).

PDO: To support the Government of the Commonwealth of Dominica in: (a) strengthening the legal and institutional framework to increase climate and disaster resilience in priority sectors; and (b) improving national fiscal capacity to respond to disaster and climate-related risks.
Description: Dominica Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Credit with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that Proclamation of Emergency has been issued to respond to a Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Credit proceeds may be withdrawn upon the issuing a Government Declaration to respond to a Disaster (as defined in Section 4.1) if there is satisfactory implementation of the program and maintenance of an adequate macroeconomic policy framework.

PDO: To enhance the Government of Costa Rica’s capacity to manage disaster risk resulting from the occurrence of natural and health related hazards and the impacts of climate change.
Description: Second Costa Rica Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a CAT DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Emergency (Estado de Energencia) has been declared by the Borrower through an executive decree to respond to an occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown trigger. Funds may be drawn upon the declaration of a State of Emergency in part or all of the Costa Rican territory, as a result of an occurring catastrophe generated by natural phenomena or health related events under terms and conditions specified in the Loan Agreement. The declaration of a State of Emergency in Costa Rica is a power of the Executive (Presidency of the Republic) in accordance with the National Emergencies and Riks Prevention Law No. 8488 of 2006 (Ley Nacional de Emergencias y Prevención del Riesgo No. 8488 de 2006). This declaration allows by way of exception, the actions, and the allocation of the necessary resources to attend to the emergency, in accordance with article 180 of the Political Constitution. As such, declarations of State of Emergency translate into emergency plans, usually with a duration of up to five years, which are developed under the responsibility of the CNE and incorporate the necessary investments to repair and rebuild damaged infrastructure and goods. The legal framework for declaring an emergency due to natural hazards and health-related events remains unchanged since the first DPL with Cat DDO and has continued to function well, including for the response to COVID-19, which was coordinated by the CNE and financed through the emergency fund mechanisms.

PDO: The proposed operation aims at supporting the GoDR’s management of risks resulting from natural and public health adverse events, by (i) strengthening disaster risk reduction in territorial planning and the housing sector, and (ii) strengthening disaster risk preparedness, response and recovery through shock-responsive social protection and fiscal risk management.
Description: Dominican Republic Second DRM Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Emergency (Estado de Emergencia) has been declared to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: (i) Drawdown triggers: similarly to the first Cat DDO, loan proceeds may be withdrawn upon a declaration of a State of Emergency by the President that has been declared to respond to an imminent or occurring adverse event, which may include either a natural or a public health-related event, 41 by means of an Executive Decree adopted in accordance with Articles 128(1)(g), 262, 265, and 26642 of the DR’s Constitution of 2015, under terms and conditions specified in the Loan Agreement. Under the first Cat DDO, the trigger worked efficiently as there was a lapse of only three working days from the declaration of a State of Emergency (related to the COVID-19 pandemic) to when the US$150 million were credited in the GoDR’s account with the Central Bank.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of the Philippines' capacity to manage disaster and climate risks, including those in the education, human settlements, and health systems.
Description: Philippines Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred-Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that (i) a proclamation declaring a State of Calamity has been issued by the Borrower’s President due to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe and/or a public health emergency; and/or (ii) a proclamation declaring a State of Public Health Emergency has been issued by the Borrower’s President due to a public health emergency.

PDO: The development objective of Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Draw Down Option Project for Somalia is to support the Federal Government of Somalia's (FGS) reform program to improve the country’s institutional, regulatory and response and preparedness capacity to manage climate and disaster risks.
Description: Somalia Disaster Risk Management DPF with a Catastrophe Deferred Draw Down Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that the Recipient’s President has declared a national emergency in accordance with Section 58 of the Recipient’s National Disaster Management Policy, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural disaster or public health emergency.

PDO: To support the Government of Romania’s efforts to strengthen inclusive multihazard preparedness and response, and to advance disaster risk reduction and climate change action.
Description: Romania Second Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Cat-DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that in response to a Catastrophe Event NCSES has issued a decision in accordance with Emergency Ordinance no. 21/2004, as subsequently amended, mobilizing financial and/or other resources to respond to such an event.

PDO: To strengthen the technical and institutional capacity of the Royal Government of Bhutan to manage the risk of climate change and natural disasters
Description: Bhutan Development Policy Credit with Second Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that in response to a Natural Disaster: (a) (i) a declaration of disaster has been made pursuant to the provisions of the Disaster Management Act; or (ii) the Prime Minister, in his capacity as the ex-officio Chairperson of the NDMA has declared a disaster; or (b) the MoF has, on the recommendation of the NDMA acting under the relevant provisions of the Disaster Management Act, issued in accordance with Section 66 of the Public Finance Act, 2007, an order to authorize use of public funds to be drawn down for the purpose of mobilizing financial resources to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.

PDO: To enhance the Government of Barbados' institutional, technical, and financial capacity to manage risk, resulting from the occurrence of natural or health-related hazards, including the adverse effects of climate change and disease outbreaks.
Description: Barbados Disaster Risk Management Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a declaration of emergency (Proclamation) has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be draw down upon the Borrower's issuance of a Proclamation pursuant to its Emergency Management Act.

PDO: The Development Objective of the DRM Cat DDO is to support the Government of Saint Lucia's management of risks resulting from hydrometeorological and public health adverse events by strengthening by (i) Integrating Climate Resilience into Physical and Data Infrastructure, and (ii) Strengthening financial resilience against hydrometeorological disasters and climate change .
Description: Saint Lucia Development Policy Credit with Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a government declaration or other executive notification has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.

PDO: The PDO is to enhance the Government of St. Vincent and the Grenadines' capacity to manage disaster and climate risks, including those in land development, education, and finance.
Description: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Disaster Risk Management Cat-DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a government declaration or other executive notification has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.

PDO: To strengthen the capacity of the Government of Nepal to manage disaster and climate risks, including disease outbreaks.
Description: Nepal Disaster and Climate Risk Management - Second Development Policy Financing with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Disaster Emergency has been declared by the Recipient either: (a) by notification in the Recipient’s gazette, as per the provisions of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act 2017, Chapter 11, section 32, in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe, or (b) upon declaration of public health emergency by the Local or Provincial Levels or the Council of Ministers, as per the provisions of the Public Health Service Act of 2018, Chapter 6, Section 48, in such manner as the Recipient deems appropriate, to respond to an imminent or occurring health-related shock.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon the issuance of either a notification in the Recipient's gazette pursuant to the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Act of 2017 or by a declaration of public health emergency by the Local or Provincial Levels or the Council of Ministers pursuant to the Health Service Act of 2018.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of Colombia’s program to manage risks resulting from climate, disaster and public health shocks at subnational level and across key sectors by (i) improving access to financial instruments and (ii) promoting resilience and climate change adaptation.
Description: Colombia - Fourth Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (Cat DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Presidential Declaration has been issued in response to a Disaster in all or part of the Borrower’s territory.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon a Presidential Declaration published in the Official Gazette (or other legal instrument compatible with the existing regulation of a state of national disaster that the Borrower may introduce from time to time with prior agreement of the Bank), addressing a "Disaster" (defined as "an imminent or occurring shock caused by one or more adverse natural or public health related events, including but not limited to: (a) disasters of natural origin caused by geological phenomena (such as earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, tsunamis, and landslides) or hydrometeorological phenomena (such as floods, droughts, forest fires, tropical cyclones, landslides, and other events related to the El Niño or La Niña phenomena); and (b) public health emergencies, such as those caused by epidemics or pandemics. Any disaster that is predominantly political, technological, security, military, or fiscal in nature is excluded from this definition.")

PDO: The Project Development Objective is to strengthen economic resilience and the technical, financial and institutional capacity to manage climate, fiscal and disaster risks
Description: United Republic of Tanzania - Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (Cat DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Second Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that: (a) the Recipient’s President has proclaimed the state of emergency or the Recipient’s Prime Minister has declared a disaster area affected by a severe emergency, in accordance with Sections 33 and 32, respectively, of the Recipient’s Disaster Management Act, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe or public health emergency; or (b) a the President of Zanzibar has declared a state of disaster in accordance with Section 31(2) of the Zanzibar Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2015, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe or public health emergency of national proportions, as defined in Section 29(2)(a) of the said Act, and (c) the event(s) on the basis of which the state of disaster has been declared meet(s) the conditions for the Association’s financing.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be drawn upon the issuance of a Presidential declaration of a natural catastrophe or emergency or health emergency pursuant to Zanzibar Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2015.

PDO: To enhance the institutional, technical, and financial capacity of the Government of Belize (GoB) to manage risk resulting from the occurrence of natural hazards, including the adverse effects of climate change.
Description: Belize Development Policy Credit with Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Government Declaration has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.

PDO: To strengthen the Government of Ecuador's program to manage risks resulting from climate, disaster, and public health shocks by (i) enhancing institutional capacity and (ii) mainstreaming resilience and climate adaptation in selected sectors.
Description: Ecuador Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Declaration of Disaster has been issued to respond to an imminent, progressive or -occurring Disaster or Catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: "Disaster or Catastrophe" means an imminent, progressive or occurring shock caused by one or more adverse natural or public health related events, including: (a) disasters of natural origin caused by geological phenomena (such as earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, tsunamis, and landslides) or hydromcteorological phenomena (such as floods, droughts, forest fires, tropical cyclones, landslides, and other events related to the El Nifio or La Nii'ia phenomena); and (b) public health emergencies, such as those caused by epidemics or pandemics, excluding any event that is political, technological, military, fiscal or securityre!ated.

PDO: The development objective of this operation is to strengthen Rwanda's preparedness and response to disaster and health emergencies, while strengthening resilience of its built environment and natural resources.
Description: Rwanda Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Credit with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that the Recipient has declared a category 3 disaster or a category 4 disaster as defined under the Disaster Management Law, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural disaster or public health emergency.

PDO: To support the Government of Guatemala in: (i) strenghtening the institutional capacity for inclusive DRM and CCA, including financial support for vulnerable groups; and (ii) mainstreaming DRM and CCA in selected sectors.
Description: Third Disaster Risk Management Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (CAT DDO)

PDO: The Program Development Objective is to strengthen Croatia’s disaster preparedness and resilience by enhancing institutional capacity at the national level and across strategic sectors, and increasing financial resilience.
Description: Croatia Disaster Preparedness and Resilience Development Policy Loan with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option (Cat DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Government Decision has been issued to respond to a Covered Event.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: “Covered Event” means, for the purpose of this Agreement, an imminent or occurring emergency situation related to natural disaster, natural catastrophe, or epidemic of an infectious disease.

PDO: i) To strengthen Samoa's macroeconomic and financial resilience ii) To increase Samoa's resilience to the effects of climate change, natural disasters, and non-communicable diseases
Description: Second Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Cat-DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Government Declaration been issued mobilizing resources to respond to a Natural Catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The Cat DDO proceeds may be drawn down after a “natural catastrophe” that results in a Proclamation of Emergency in accordance with Article 105 of the Constitution of Samoa and when the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that such a government proclamation or declaration has been issued. For the purpose of this operation, a natural catastrophe includes emergencies due to cyclones, volcanic eruptions, tsunami, floods, earthquakes, high winds and seasonal droughts and excludes any emergency due to military, economic, political or technological causes.

PDO: To support the government’s efforts to: (i) strengthen public financial management; (ii) enhance infrastructure management, and disaster-and climate-resilience; and (iii) improve social protection and inclusion in education.
Description: Tuvalu First Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that in response to a Natural Disaster, the Recipient’s Head of State has declared that a State of Public Emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory, or in part of the Recipient’s territory in relation to such Natural Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The proposed drawdown trigger is governed by the National Disaster Management Act of 2007 [Cap. 20.38]. The Act defines a disaster as the actual or imminent occurrence of an event which endangers or threatens the safety or health of any communities or persons in Tuvalu, or which destroys or damages, any property in Tuvalu. The Act further provides that the Head of State (acting in accordance with the advice of the Prime Minister) may by proclamation declare that a state of public emergency exists in Tuvalu, or in a part of Tuvalu, if circumstances of a disaster: (1) appear likely to become beyond the scope, provisions and resources of the National Disaster Plan4; (2) exceed the capabilities of the affected community to deal with the disaster. In view of the relevant provisions of the laws described above, the proposed drawdown trigger for this operation is as follows: In response to a Natural Disaster,5 the Recipient’s Head of State has declared, in accordance with the Recipient’s applicable law, that a State of Public Emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory, or in part of the Recipient’s territory in relation to such Natural Disaster.

PDO: To: i) promote private sector-led economic recovery; ii) enhance climate, disaster and social resilience; and iii) strengthen debt and public financial management
Description: Fiji First Economic Reform and Climate Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option (CAT-DDO)
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Natural Disaster has been declared by the Recipient’s Cabinet on advice of the National Disaster Management Council or of the Emergency Committee, should the National Disaster Management Council not be able to meet, in accordance with Sections 17 (1) and 17 (2) of the Natural Disaster Management Act to respond to an imminent or occurring Natural Catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Drawdown trigger: The Cat-DDO proceeds may be drawn down when the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Declaration of Natural Disaster10 has been declared by the Cabinet on advice of the National Disaster Management Council11 in accordance with Article 17(1)12 of the Natural Disaster Management Act in response to a “Natural Catastrophe”. For the purpose of this operation, “Natural Catastrophe” is defined as “an imminent or occurring emergency situation at national scale, caused by an earthquake, tsunami, cyclone, high winds, storm, heavy rain, flood, volcanic eruption, drought, bush fire or any other natural hazard, or any public health emergency, that requires Fiji to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources, but excluding man-made disasters”.

PDO: To promote an inclusive economic response and recovery from the impacts of COVID-19, strengthen Samoa’s macro-fiscal resilience, and increase Samoa’s resilience to climate change, natural disasters, and health risks.
Description: Samoa First Response, Recovery and Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe Deferr
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Proclamation of Emergency has been issued to respond to a Natural Catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The Cat-DDO proceeds may be drawn down after a “natural catastrophe” that results in a Proclamation of Emergency in accordance with Article 105 of the Constitution of Samoa and when the International Development Association (the “Association”) is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that such a Government proclamation has been issued. In the event that the Head of State is unavailable to make a Proclamation of Emergency under Article 105 of the Constitution, the Council of Deputies will make such a proclamation on the Head of State’s behalf in accordance with Article 23 of the Constitution. For the purpose of this operation and drawing on Article 105 and the DEMA 2007, a “natural catastrophe” includes imminent or occurring emergencies caused by an earthquake, tsunami, volcanic eruption, cyclone, high winds, storm, heavy rainfall, flood, drought, bush fire or other natural hazard, or any public health-related event, that requires Government to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources.

PDO: To: (i) strengthen public finances; (ii) enhance resilience to climate change, natural disasters, and health-related risks; and (iii) support economic recovery and improved labor market outcomes.
Description: Tonga Second Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency has been declared by the Recipient’s Prime Minister or other authorized official, in accordance with the manner prescribed in Part IV Section 32 of the Recipient’s Emergency Management Act 2007 or other applicable law which replaces such act, to respond to a Catastrophic Event.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: The drawdown trigger has been designed to be applicable under both the Act and the Bill. For the purpose of this operation, “Catastrophic Event” means an imminent or occurring emergency situation, caused by: (i) a cyclone, flood, drought, storm, storm surge, tsunami, earthquake, volcanic eruption or other similar event; or (ii) an epidemic, pandemic, or other public health emergency, that requires the Recipient to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources, but excluding an emergency situation caused by human induced hazard.

PDO: To support the government’s efforts to: (i) strengthen public financial management; (ii) enhance infrastructure management, and disaster- and climate-resilience; and (iii) improve social protection and inclusion in education.
Description: Tuvalu Second Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it that, in response to a Natural Disaster, the Recipient’s Head of State has declared, in accordance with the Recipient’s applicable law, that a State of Public Emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory, or in part of the Recipient’s territory, in relation to such Natural Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: In response to a Natural Disaster, the Recipient’s Head of State has declared, in accordance with the Recipient’s applicable law, that a State of Public Emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory, or in part of the Recipient’s territory in relation to such Natural Disaster.

PDO: To support the Government’s efforts to strengthen the foundations for a resilient and equitable economic recovery through (i) reducing fiscal risks and improving debt transparency; (ii) strengthening the resilience of poor and vulnerable households to climate shocks; and (iii) enabling a sustainable private sector-led recovery.
Description: Cabo Verde: Second Sustainable and Equitable Recovery DPF with a Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche for Credit (C) unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a government declaration or other executive notification has been issued or a State of Calamity to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Credit proceeds will be disbursed if the pre-specified trigger defined in the financing agreement is met and will not be tied to any specific purchase. Once the operation is declared effective, if drawdown conditions for a natural catastrophe are met and a "State of Calamity” is declared, MoF may submit a withdrawal application requesting the World Bank to deposit the proceeds of the Credit into the budget account that forms part of the country's official foreign exchange reserves held with the Central Bank and acceptable to the World Bank. Further information of the drawdown triggers, financial features, drawdown period, and renewals are detailed in the financing and loan agreements. The Government will ensure that upon receipt of the Credit proceeds, an equivalent amount will be credited in the Government's budget management system to finance budgeted expenditures.

PDO: To: (i) enhance private sector-led growth, (ii) boost domestic revenue collection, and (iii) strengthen social and climate resilience
Description: Benin Boosting Inclusive Growth and Resilience DPF1 series with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the CAT-DDO Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a declaration of a state of disaster or health emergency made pursuant to Article 6 of the Recipient’s Decret N° 2023 – 334, dated June 21, 2023, has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural disaster or health emergency.

PDO: Support The Gambia’s efforts to (i) unlock its productive potential; (ii) strengthen procurement, economic governance of SOEs, and domestic revenue mobilization; and (iii) strengthen climate and social resilience.
Description: Gambia Boosting Resilience and Unlocking Productive Potential DPF1 with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the CAT-DDO Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a declaration of a state of public emergency made pursuant to the National Disaster Management Act, 2008, has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural disaster including a health emergency.

PDO: To strengthen the foundations for: (i) sound fiscal and debt management; and (ii) a competitive and climate-resilient economy.
Description: Mauritania Fiscal Management and Resilience Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Cat-DDO Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that the state of catastrophe in a specified geographic area and for a specified period has been declared further to the occurrence of a degree 1 emergency, whether it is caused by a natural catastrophe or a public health related event, all in accordance with the provision of the Recipient’s decree no. 2023-142 to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe.

PDO: To: (i) promote climate and disaster resilient fiscal policy and trade facilitation, and (ii) improve the institutional and regulatory framework for climate and disaster resilient infrastructure.
Description: Tuvalu First Inclusive Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that in response to a Natural Disaster, the Recipient’s Head of State has declared, in accordance with the Recipient’s applicable law, that a State of Public Emergency exists in the Recipient’s territory, or in part of the Recipient’s territory, in relation to such Natural Disaster.

PDO: To support the Government of Fiji in its efforts to build fiscal, climate and disaster resilience, and the foundations for private sector development
Description: Fiji Growth and Resilience First DPF with a Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of disaster has been declared by the Recipient’s Cabinet in accordance with Sections 17 (1) and (2) of the Natural Disaster Management Act, or other applicable law which replaces such act, to respond to a Catastrophic Event.

PDO: To: i) strengthen fiscal and debt sustainability. ii) enhance resilience to climate change and disasters.
Description: Tonga Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe-Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of emergency has been declared by the Recipient’s Prime Minister or other authorized official, in accordance with the manner prescribed in Part IV Section 32 of the Recipient’s Emergency Management Act 2007 or other applicable law which replaces such act, to respond to a Catastrophic Event.

PDO: To support reforms aimed at 1) anchoring fiscal sustainability and transparency, 2) stimulating private sector-led growth, and 3) strengthening the resilience of the poor against shocks.
Description: Malawi First Growth and Resilience DPO with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made under Category 2 (the CAT DDO Withdrawal Tranche for Grant (B)) unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that: (a) the President of the Recipient has declared a “State of Disaster” in accordance with Section 37 of the Disaster Risk Management Act of 2023, or such other law applicable to DRM as may be in force in the Recipient’s territory at the time; and (b) there is an imminent or occurring: (i) natural disaster as a result of natural geological phenomena, including but not limited to earthquakes, volcanoes and landslides, hydro-meteorological phenomena such as floods, droughts, tropical cyclones, and El Niño/La Niña events; or (ii) health disaster as a result of a plague or epidemic of disease that threatens the life or wellbeing of a community.

PDO: Are: (i) strengthening fiscal management, and (ii) promoting inclusive and climate-resilient economic development.
Description: Kiribati First Resilient Growth Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
