Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Disaster Declaration has been issued by the Recipient’s President, in accordance with the manner prescribed in Section 21(1) and Section 21(2) of the Recipient’s Disaster Risk Management and Climate Change Act 2019, to respond to a Disaster Event.

PDO: The proposed series of two operations supports the Solomon Islands Government in its efforts to improve fiscal management, private sector development, and climate and disaster resilience.
Description: The First Solomon Islands Resilience DPO with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it that, in order to respond to a Natural Disaster: (a) a state of public emergency has been declared by the Recipient’s Governor-General in accordance with the manner prescribed in Section 16 of the Recipient’s Constitution; or (b) a state of disaster has been declared by the authorized official of the Recipient in accordance with Section 12 of the National Disaster Council Act.

PDO: To: (i) enhance fiscal management; and (ii) build disaster and climate resilience
Description: RMI Enhancing Sustainability and Resilience Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a state of disaster has been declared by the Recipient’s Cabinet or President, in accordance with the manner prescribed in Part IV of the Recipient’s Disaster Risk Management Act, 2023, to respond to a Catastrophic Event.

PDO: To: (i) enhance fiscal management and resilience, (ii) promote private-sector investment and resilience in enabling sectors, and (iii) strengthen disaster risk management and climate resilience.
Description: First Zambia Recovery and Resilience Programmatic DPF with CAT-DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made under the First Withdrawal Tranche, unless the Association is satisfied: (a) with the Program being carried out by the Recipient; and (b) with the adequacy of the macroeconomic framework. 2. No withdrawal shall be made under the Second Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that: (a) the President of the Recipient has declared a “State of Disaster” in accordance with Section 36 of the Disaster Management Act of 2010, or such other law applicable to DRM, as discussed and agreed with the Association, as may be in force in Zambia at the time; and (b) there is an imminent or occurring: (i) natural disaster as a result of natural geological phenomena, including but not limited to earthquakes, volcanoes, and landslides, hydro-meteorological phenomena such as floods, droughts, tropical cyclones, and El Niño / La Niña events; or (ii) health disaster as a result of a plague or epidemic of disease that threatens the life or well-being of a community.

PDO: To 1) promote fiscal resilience and transparency, and 2) strengthen climate and disaster risk management.
Description: Vanuatu Resilience Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that: (a) a state of emergency has been declared by the Recipient’s President, in accordance with the manner prescribed in Article 69(b) of the Constitution or section 32 of the Disaster Risk Management Act No. 23 of 2019 (as amended), to respond to a Natural Catastrophe; or (b) the Recipient’s Minister of Health has, in accordance with the manner prescribed in sections 21A and 21B of the Public Health (Amendment) Act No. 3 of 2021, issued an order to declare that a state of public health emergency exists in all or in parts of the Recipient’s territory.

PDO: The program development objectives are to (i) improve fiscal management; (ii) enable conditions for inclusive growth; and (iii) build climate and disaster resilience.
Description: Sierra Leone First Macro Stability and Resilience DPO with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made for Grant B under Category 2 (Cat DDO Withdrawal Tranche) unless: (i) the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that: (1) the President of the Republic of Sierra Leone has issued a proclamation of a state of public emergency in accordance with the Constitution, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe or public health emergency; or (2) a government declaration or other executive notification has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe or public health emergency; and (ii) the event(s) on the basis for which the state of emergency or public health emergency has been declared meet(s) the conditions for the Association’s financing.

PDO: The program development objective aims to bolster inclusive growth and resilience by (i) strengthening fiscal resilience, (ii) enabling foundations for private sector development, and (iii) supporting social, disaster, and climate resilience.
Description: Liberia First Resilient and Inclusive Growth Development Policy Financing
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made for Credit C under Category 2 (Cat DDO Withdrawal Tranche) unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that: (a) the President of the Recipient has proclaimed and declared the existence of a state of emergency in the Recipient or any part thereof, in accordance with Article 86 of the Recipient’s Constitution, and Section 1.3 of the NDMA Act, 2012, or such other applicable law as may be in force in the Recipient’s territory at the time, to respond to an imminent or occurring natural catastrophe or a public health emergency has been proclaimed by the Minister of Health as a result of a plague or epidemic of disease declared according to chapter 14 or other relevant chapter of the Public Health Law, 2017; and (b) the Association has confirmed that the event(s) on the basis of which the state of emergency or public health emergency has been declared meet(s) the conditions for the Association’s financing.

PDO: (i) To strengthen fiscal sustainability through improved fiscal management tools and spending effiency; and (ii) To enhance resilience to natural disasters and climate change.
Description: El Salvador Fiscal Sustainability and Resilience DPF with Cat DDO
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a State of Emergency Declaration has been issued to respond to a Disaster in all or part of the Borrower�s territory.

PDO: (i) To facilitate trade, digital adoption and financial stability for private sector development; and (ii) To enhance fiscal and physical resilience to climate change and disasters, including natural hazard and health shocks.
Description: Jamaica - Sustainable and Resilient Recovery DPF with CAT DDO
Note: Had a supplemental financing on the DPO component of 42 mil.
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Bank is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Government Declaration has been issued by the Borrower to respond to an imminent or occurring Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: Funds may be draw down upon a "Government Declaration" (i.e., a declaration " issued by the Borrower’s Prime Minister and published in the Borrower’s Government Gazette, that a Disaster has occurred or is imminent in the territory of the Borrower or any part thereof, pursuant to the provisions of the Borrower’s Disaster Risk Management Act). For the purposes of the Government Declaration, "Disaster" is defined as " an imminent or occurring situation due to a natural or health disaster, including but not limited to earthquakes, hurricanes, storms, floods, fires, outbreak of pestilence or of infectious disease or other calamity, as a result of which widespread dislocation of essential services are likely to occur and to require emergency intervention by the Borrower, in accordance with the Disaster Risk Management Act. For clarity, any emergency that is predominantly political, technological, security, industrial, military, or fiscal in nature, is excluded."

PDO: To support the GoSVG in: (i) responding to the COVID-19 pandemic to protect the vulnerable; (ii) strengthening fiscal resilience; and (iii) enhancing climate and disaster resilience
Description: Second Fiscal Reform and Resilience Development Policy Credit with a Cat DDO
Global Practice: Environment, Natural Resources & the Blue Economy
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Disaster Declaration Document has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring Disaster.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: In accordance with the NEDM Act, the Prime Minister may declare that a “disaster for the state, or a part of the state” has occurred; after which the Attorney General’s Office would produce a legal instrument of declaration that specifies whether the disaster is national or local. This legal instrument will be provided to the Bank. Declarations of disasters have been issued previously in 2010 and 2013. - In accordance with the Constitution (1979, amended 1988), the Governor General may, “by proclamation which shall be published in the Official Gazette, declare that a state of emergency exists if the Governor is satisfied that a public emergency has arisen as a result of the occurrence of any volcanic eruption, earthquake, hurricane, flood, fire, outbreak of pestilence or of infectious disease, or any other calamity similar to the foregoing.” - These declarations shall be done via a Disaster Notification Service or a formal announcement via national broadcast and evidenced in the form of a copy of the Disaster Declaration Document.

PDO: The objectives and pillars of the operation are to support the Government of Kiribati’s (GOK) reforms to: (i) strengthen fiscal management, and (ii) promote inclusive and climate-resilient economic development.
Description: Kiribati Second Resilient Growth Development Policy Operation with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option

PDO: To support the Government of Papua New Guinea reforms to: (i) promote fiscal sustainability; (ii) strengthen trade facilitation and market access; and (iii) improve resilience to climate change, natural disasters and health emergencies.
Description: Papua New Guinea Growth and Resilience Development Policy Financing with a Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option
Trigger from Loan/Financing Agreements: No withdrawal shall be made of the Single Withdrawal Tranche unless the Association is satisfied, based on evidence satisfactory to it, that a Declaration of a National Emergency has been issued to respond to an imminent or occurring Catastrophic Event.
Additional Information from Minutes & Pr: "Catastrophic Event" means, for the purposes of this Agreement, an imminent or occurring emergency situation caused by an earthquake, tsunami, volcanic eruption, cyclone, high winds, stonn, heavy rainfall, flood, drought, bush fire or other natural hazard, or any public health-related event, that requires the Recipient to promptly mobilize its capacity and/or financial resources, but excluding an emergency situation caused by human induced hazard.
